deck.name: Stacked Aggro 4×5
deck.rationale: Stacked deck of 4 copies × 5 cheap-cost (≤2 energy) basic attackers + 40 basic energy. Built to maximise prize-take frequency, validating that the simulator terminates correctly.

matches.label: random_vs_random_stacked_deck
matches.note: Reproduced from submission/_diagnose_termination.py — same random-policy match measured during root-cause analysis. With a stacked deck the simulator terminates 87.5% of the time, KOs and prize takes occur normally.

matches.label: agent_vs_random_stacked_deck
matches.reason: MCTS-driven decisions at 8+ iterations cost 70-250 ms each on CPU. Stacked-deck games take 200-400 actions per game. 15+ games of agent-vs-random exceeded the available compute budget. The agent's behaviour and per-decision latency ARE measured (see submission/csv/agent_vs_random.csv) on the original default-deck pairing — 30 games at 30 MCTS iter, 252 ms mean per decision.
